100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 10, 1926, the British Empire celebrated its first Empire Day, marking a day of unity among its nations. In the United States, the ongoing debates about Prohibition continued to stir public sentiment, with increasing calls for reform. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions escalated as the Weimar Republic faced challenges from both left and right factions. These events reflect a world grappling with national identity and governance in the post-World War I era.
Updated: Wednesday, June 10, 2026 at 09:42:30 AM CDT
